# Meta data

<pre>
Ramon benchmark for Z3
-
Job description: QF_LIA, weighted scheduling, MILD cube bias 2:1 (cube/lcube 2048, others 1024)
Job tag: lia_w_cube2x_qflia_60_rs_603
Runner: rise-runner-2
Z3 repo: Z3Prover/z3
Z3 commit: 31059174f20c7c2c8ecb8880e22e12326251c54a
Z3 branch: lia_w
Z3 options: "-T:60 smt.random_seed=603 lp.lia_weighted_scheduling=true lp.lia_weight_cube=2048 lp.lia_weight_lcube=2048 lp.lia_weight_hnf=1024 lp.lia_weight_gomory=1024 lp.lia_weight_dio=1024"
Z3 inputs: https://zenodo.org/records/16740866/files/QF_LIA.tar.zst?download=1
Z3 commit message: lp: make weighted-scheduling base weights configurable

Expose the per-method base weights of the weighted integer-solving
scheduler as lp parameters (lia_weight_cube, lia_weight_lcube,
lia_weight_hnf, lia_weight_gomory, lia_weight_dio) so weight
distributions can be A/B benchmarked without recompiling.

Defaults preserve the prior hardcoded policy (4096 for the cheap
cube/lcube methods, 1024 for the post-move hnf/gomory/dio methods).
Setting all five equal yields an unbiased roulette; raising the cut/dio
weights favors the methods that close UNSAT instances.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

</pre>
